Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. The Collective Ambition Behind Odysseus, a Game-Changing Sci-Fi Larp 87 upvotes, 3 comments
  2. LibreOffice: Request to the European Commission to adhere to its own guidances 210 upvotes, 32 comments
  3. FrameBook 216 upvotes, 38 comments
  4. LibreOffice Writer now supports Markdown 112 upvotes, 25 comments
  5. Rijksmuseum researchers discover new painting by Rembrandt van Rijn 121 upvotes, 28 comments
  6. How to run Qwen 3.5 locally 396 upvotes, 132 comments
  7. What if the Apple ][ had run on Field-Sequential? 46 upvotes, 10 comments
  8. I made a programming language with M&Ms 8 upvotes, 0 comments
  9. I ported Linux to the PS5 and turned it into a Steam Machine 259 upvotes, 104 comments
  10. SWE-CI: Evaluating Agent Capabilities in Maintaining Codebases via CI 101 upvotes, 35 comments
  11. CLI RSS/Atom feed reader inspired by Taskwarrior, synced using Git 47 upvotes, 14 comments
  12. Cloud VM benchmarks 2026 313 upvotes, 139 comments
  13. The changing goalposts of AGI and timelines 281 upvotes, 140 comments
  14. Notes on writing Rust-based Wasm 157 upvotes, 73 comments
  15. Log messages are mostly for the people operating your software 48 upvotes, 20 comments
  16. Apple's 512GB Mac Studio vanishes, a quiet acknowledgment of the RAM shortage 322 upvotes, 196 comments
  17. Beagle, a source code management system that stores AST trees 61 upvotes, 31 comments
  18. WSL Manager 9 upvotes, 2 comments
  19. AI doesn't replace white collar work 11 upvotes, 4 comments
  20. Why can't you tune your guitar? (2019) 107 upvotes, 92 comments
  21. My Homelab Setup 66 upvotes, 54 comments
  22. Google just gave Sundar Pichai a $692M pay package 79 upvotes, 69 comments
  23. Claude struggles to cope with ChatGPT exodus 77 upvotes, 67 comments
  24. In vitro neurons learn and exhibit sentience when embodied in a game-world(2022) 7 upvotes, 2 comments
  25. Living human brain cells play DOOM on a CL1 [video] 67 upvotes, 61 comments
  26. A basket of new fruit varieties is coming your way 10 upvotes, 7 comments
  27. Show HN: I built a real-time OSINT dashboard pulling 15 live global feeds 6 upvotes, 3 comments
  28. How Big Diaper absorbs billions of extra dollars from American parents 105 upvotes, 170 comments
  29. Show HN: Skir – like Protocol Buffer but better 17 upvotes, 19 comments
  30. The new Apple begins to emerge 48 upvotes, 79 comments

Last generated 3:30am, 9 March 2026. Source here.
